title: Xenial Comforting Algerian Vegetable Tagine
description: A floral and crumbly Algerian dish featuring a vegetable stock cube and ferment-kimchi, perfect for a comforting meal. This tagine is also FODMAP-friendly.

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: minced, use a small amount for FODMAP-friendliness

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the vegetable stock cube, ferment-kimchi, carrots, zucchini, chickpeas, dried apricots, cumin, and paprika to the pot.
Stir well to combine, then bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 30 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
